summary: Microsoft documents menu show delay through SystemParametersInfo with SPI_GETMENUSHOWDELAY and SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY. A local runtime diff on 2026-03-14 confirmed that calling SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY with 400 persisted HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\MenuShowDelay = "400", while calling SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY with 0 persisted MenuShowDelay = "0". The current app write of MenuShowDelay = "0" therefore matches the observed persisted backend for the documented Win32 feature surface on this build.

setting.name: Remove Menu Show Delay
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can wait a short moment before showing some menus. Setting the delay lower can make menus feel faster and more immediate.
setting.why_people_change_it: People reduce menu delay when they want faster-feeling desktop interactions and less waiting before menus appear.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: A lower menu delay can feel snappier, but menus can appear more abruptly. This record is validated through the official Win32 menu-delay API plus a local API-to-registry runtime diff, not through a Microsoft-published registry contract for MenuShowDelay.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents SPI_GETMENUSHOWDELAY and SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY as the supported feature surface and states that the value is a delay in milliseconds. On 2026-03-14, a local runtime diff showed SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY 400 persisting MenuShowDelay = "400" and SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY 0 persisting MenuShowDelay = "0" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop, with the original local state restored afterward. That validates MenuShowDelay as the observed persisted backend for the public Win32 feature surface on this build.

allowed_values.label: No menu show delay
allowed_values.meaning: A local SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY runtime diff confirmed that setting the official Win32 menu-delay feature to 0 persists MenuShowDelay = "0" on this build.

allowed_values.label: Default-like menu delay
allowed_values.meaning: A local SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY runtime diff confirmed that setting the official Win32 menu-delay feature to 400 persists MenuShowDelay = "400" on this build.

targets.notes: Validated as an API-backed runtime registry mapping. No Microsoft page captured in this pass publishes MenuShowDelay directly as the supported registry contract.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes MenuShowDelay = "0" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop. A local runtime diff confirmed that this matches the persisted zero-delay state produced by the official Win32 menu-delay API on this build.

windows_defaults.label: Observed local baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles where MenuShowDelay is present

states.rationale: 400 ms is the commonly observed local baseline captured in the runtime diff and represents the non-zero delayed menu state on this machine.

windows_defaults.notes: This record validates the persisted 0 and 400 states. It does not claim that all Windows installs default to 400 or that an absent-value default was captured in this pass.

recommended_profiles.label: Remove menu show delay
intended_for: Users who want faster-feeling menu interactions
People who prefer immediate menu display
avoid_for: Users who prefer a softer, slightly delayed menu feel

states.rationale: Set the observed persisted registry backend to the zero-delay state proven through SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want menus to open immediately.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer a small built-in delay before menus appear.
tradeoffs: The interface can feel more abrupt.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: SystemParametersInfo - menu show delay actions
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents SPI_GETMENUSHOWDELAY and SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY as the public Win32 surface for reading and changing the menu show delay in milliseconds.

evidence.title: Local SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY runtime diff - MenuShowDelay persistence
evidence.summary: A local runtime diff called SPI_SETMENUSHOWDELAY with 400 and 0, then read both SPI_GETMENUSHOWDELAY and HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\MenuShowDelay after each change. Setting 400 produced API 400 with registry value "400". Setting 0 produced API 0 with registry value "0". The original local state was restored at the end.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes MenuShowDelay = "0" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop.

decision.why: The menu-delay feature semantics are officially documented through the Win32 API, and the runtime diff proves that MenuShowDelay is the persisted backend for the tested delayed and zero-delay states on this build. The app writes the same zero-delay state and supports restoring the previous value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: after_slow: API 400, registry MenuShowDelay = "400". after_fast: API 0, registry MenuShowDelay = "0". restored: API 0, registry MenuShowDelay = "0".
validation_proof.notes: The local runtime diff used the official SystemParametersInfo menu-delay API to change the feature and then read the persisted registry backend. The machine ended in its original state.
